data.title: NeoVolta Expands Global Reach With Potisedge Equity Agreement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NeoVolta ( (NEOV) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 15, 2026, NeoVolta Power, NeoVolta and Can Current Corporation amended and restated the operating agreement for NeoVolta Power, removing NPJV MANAGER LLC as a member, increasing NeoVolta’s Class A equity stake, reducing and reallocating Class B units solely to CCC, and consolidating board control under NeoVolta while granting CCC non‑voting observer rights tied to technical services. That same day they also amended the contribution agreement to remove NMC, and NeoVolta Power agreed to buy $9 million of CCC’s battery storage manufacturing equipment under a milestone‑based asset purchase, strengthening the Georgia facility’s build‑out and NeoVolta’s operational control of the venture.

On April 20, 2026, NeoVolta entered a management services agreement with Singapore‑based Potisedge Technology to provide sales and marketing coordination for its commercial and industrial battery storage business in exchange for up to 1.2 million NeoVolta common shares vesting over 24 months. The equity‑based compensation, structured with forfeiture and accelerated vesting provisions upon certain terminations, is intended to align Potisedge’s incentives with NeoVolta’s growth objectives and deepen its international commercial capabilities.

The most recent analyst rating on (NEOV) stock is a Buy with a $7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NeoVolta stock, see the NEOV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NEOV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NEOV is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance—large losses and significant cash burn despite manageable leverage. Technicals add pressure with the stock trading below key moving averages and a negative MACD. Valuation is hard to justify with negative earnings and no dividend, while corporate events are mixed due to dilution from the equity raise and leadership turnover.

To see Spark’s full report on NEOV stock, click here.

More about NeoVolta

NeoVolta Inc. is a Nevada-based company focused on commercial and industrial battery energy storage, with operations tied to a domestic manufacturing facility in Georgia through its NeoVolta Power, LLC joint venture. The company’s strategy centers on owning and operating battery energy storage manufacturing capacity in the U.S. while leveraging technical and marketing partnerships to expand its market presence.

Average Trading Volume: 821,074

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $140.5M

data.title: FiscalNote Gains Temporary Debt Relief After NYSE Delisting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FiscalNote Holdings ( (NOTE) ) has issued an update.

FiscalNote Holdings, Inc. saw its Class A common stock and warrants delisted from the New York Stock Exchange after the NYSE filed a Form 25 with the SEC on April 3, 2026, with the delisting becoming effective on April 13, 2026. The securities now trade on the OTCID Basic Market under the symbols NOTE and NOTEW, and the company has indicated it plans to seek listing on alternative tiers or exchanges to improve investor access and liquidity as it advances its strategic plans.

Following the delisting, FiscalNote entered into forbearance agreements on April 21, 2026, with subordinated creditors GPO FN Noteholder, LLC and YA II PN, Ltd, who agreed to waive defaults under subordinated convertible debt triggered by the delisting and to refrain from exercising related rights until May 21, 2026. The agreements provide temporary relief on debt covenant breaches, aiming to stabilize the company’s capital structure and preserve operational flexibility while it navigates the transition to OTC trading and explores new listing options.

The most recent analyst rating on (NOTE) stock is a Buy with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FiscalNote Holdings stock, see the NOTE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NOTE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NOTE is a Underperform.

The score is pressured primarily by weak financial performance (persistent negative operating/free cash flow and a sharp profitability deterioration in 2025) and a deeply bearish technical setup (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation provides limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend, and corporate events add downside risk from delisting/OTC transition despite some constructive strategy announcements.

To see Spark’s full report on NOTE stock, click here.

More about FiscalNote Holdings

FiscalNote Holdings, Inc. operates in the information and data services industry, providing technology-driven policy and regulatory intelligence. Its primary products include data platforms and analytical tools that help businesses and institutions track, interpret, and respond to legislative and regulatory developments, with a market focus on clients requiring real-time policy insights.

PPG Industries ( (PPG) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 15, 2026, PPG’s board compensation committee approved new performance-based Market Stock Unit awards for certain executive officers, notably granting $1.5 million target MSU awards to K. Henrik Bergstrom and Kevin Braun while excluding CEO Timothy Knavish and retiring CFO Vincent Morales. The MSUs, which vest on May 8, 2029, tie payouts directly to three-year absolute stock price performance and an earnings-per-share threshold, reinforcing pay-for-performance discipline and executive retention while linking leadership rewards to shareholder returns.

At the April 16, 2026 annual meeting, shareholders re-elected 12 directors, approved advisory executive compensation, ratified PricewaterhouseCoopers as the 2026 auditor, and backed a new omnibus incentive plan, signaling broad support for PPG’s governance and pay structures. A shareholder proposal to require an independent board chair failed, preserving the current leadership framework and indicating investor acceptance of the combined chair and CEO role.

The most recent analyst rating on (PPG) stock is a Hold with a $127.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on PPG Industries stock, see the PPG Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Rigel Regains Rights as Eli Lilly Ends Collaboration
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rigel ( (RIGL) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 16, 2026, Rigel Pharmaceuticals received notice that Eli Lilly is terminating their 2021 license and collaboration agreement, which will become effective June 15, 2026. The deal had granted Lilly exclusive global rights to develop and commercialize ocadusertib and other RIPK1 inhibitors for non-CNS and CNS diseases, along with joint development activities and potential milestone and royalty payments to Rigel.

Upon termination, Lilly’s rights to the licensed compounds will cease, subject to transition provisions, and Rigel expects to regain full rights to ocadusertib and related programs. Following this move and the earlier November 2025 termination of the CNS program, Rigel does not expect future milestones or royalties from the agreement and is currently evaluating the impact on its business and development strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (RIGL) stock is a Buy with a $57.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rigel stock, see the RIGL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RIGL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RIGL is a Outperform.

The score is supported most by improving fundamentals (profitability, revenue growth, stronger cash generation and balance sheet) and very low P/E valuation. Offsetting this are weak technicals (downtrend and negative momentum signals) and earnings-quality/execution risks highlighted on the earnings call, including tax-benefit-driven GAAP income and potential non-recurring growth tailwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on RIGL stock, click here.

More about Rigel

Rigel Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering, developing and commercializing small-molecule therapies. Its pipeline includes investigational kinase inhibitors such as ocadusertib, a potent and selective RIPK1 inhibitor being developed for the treatment of various immune-inflammatory and related non-CNS and CNS diseases.

Bridgewater Bancshares reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 21, 2026, with net income rising to $17.4 million, or $0.58 per diluted share, up from both the prior quarter and a year earlier, while adjusted EPS was $0.41. The bank expanded its net interest margin to 2.99%, improved funding costs, grew loans and core deposits, strengthened capital ratios and asset quality, and declared a June 1, 2026 cash dividend on its 5.875% Series A preferred stock, while also launching a $50 million at-the-market common stock offering to support future growth and balance-sheet efficiency.

data.title: National Health Investors Divests Skilled Nursing Portfolio to NHC
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

National Health Investors ( (NHI) ) just unveiled an update.

National Health Investors announced on April 21, 2026 that it entered a purchase and sale agreement to divest a portfolio of 32 skilled nursing facilities and three independent living facilities to current lessee National HealthCare Corporation for $560 million, with closing anticipated on July 1, 2026, subject to customary conditions. The deal, approved by a special committee of non-interested directors, is designed to cut skilled nursing exposure, boost private-pay senior housing concentration, strengthen the balance sheet by reducing leverage and increasing liquidity, and expand capital recycling capacity as the company redirects proceeds toward debt repayment and future senior housing investments.

The 35 properties generated about $39.7 million in cash lease revenue in 2025, and the expected redeployment of sale proceeds, potentially including tax-deferred reinvestment, is intended to support NHI’s long-term capital allocation strategy and governance enhancements by removing conflicts tied to overlapping relationships with NHC. Management signaled that updated financial guidance, reflecting the timing and impact of the transaction and capital redeployment, will be provided with first-quarter 2026 earnings, underscoring the transaction’s importance for the REIT’s portfolio mix and financial profile.

The most recent analyst rating on (NHI) stock is a Hold with a $86.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on National Health Investors stock, see the NHI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NHI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NHI is a Outperform.

To see Spark’s full report on NHI stock, click here.

More about National Health Investors

National Health Investors, Inc. is a self-managed real estate investment trust focused on owning, leasing, operating and financing high-quality senior housing communities and medical facilities. Established in 1991, the Maryland-based REIT operates through Real Estate Investments and SHOP segments, with a portfolio that includes independent and assisted living facilities, entrance-fee communities, senior living campuses, skilled nursing facilities and hospitals.

data.title: Foghorn Highlights Preclinical Chromatin Oncology Advances at AACR
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Foghorn Therapeutics ( (FHTX) ).

On April 21, 2026, Foghorn Therapeutics announced preclinical data for several oncology programs presented at the 2026 American Association for Cancer Research Annual Meeting, including its selective SMARCA2 inhibitor FHD-909 and selective CBP, EP300 and ARID1B degrader candidates. The data underscore the company’s strategy of exploiting chromatin biology and synthetic lethal relationships in genetically defined cancers, reinforcing its positioning as a leader in targeting chromatin regulators and supporting the value of its Lilly partnership and broader precision-oncology pipeline.

The company also furnished an updated April 2026 investor presentation outlining a broad pipeline of first-in-class precision therapeutics, multi-billion-dollar market opportunities and its progress in advancing FHD-909 through a Phase 1 trial while moving additional degrader programs toward IND-enabling studies. With substantial non-dilutive funding from Lilly and a strengthened balance sheet after a January 2026 financing, Foghorn appears well-capitalized to progress its chromatin-focused drugs, a development that is significant for investors watching emerging approaches to “undruggable” cancer targets.

The most recent analyst rating on (FHTX) stock is a Buy with a $13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Foghorn Therapeutics stock, see the FHTX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FHTX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FHTX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (large ongoing losses, heavy cash burn, and negative equity) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include constructive corporate updates—added financing and extended runway plus clinical progress and leadership strengthening—while valuation offers limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on FHTX stock, click here.

More about Foghorn Therapeutics

Foghorn Therapeutics Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on precision oncology, using its Gene Traffic Control Platform to target chromatin biology and previously undruggable epigenetic regulators. Its pipeline includes enzyme inhibitors and protein degraders such as the SMARCA2 inhibitor FHD-909 and selective CBP, EP300 and ARID1B degraders, addressing genetically defined cancers including SMARCA4- and ARID1A-mutant tumors.

The company has a major strategic collaboration with Eli Lilly, which includes a 50/50 U.S. economic split on two lead programs and tiered ex-U.S. royalties, and received $380 million in upfront consideration from that deal. As of December 31, 2025, Foghorn reported $158.9 million in cash and equivalents, supplemented by $50 million raised in January 2026, providing a cash runway into the first half of 2028 and supporting advancement of multiple oncology programs.

data.title: Virgin Galactic Enhances Executive Protections Amid Potential Transitions
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Virgin Galactic Holdings ( (SPCE) ).

On April 21, 2026, Virgin Galactic amended employment agreements with its Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, Douglas Ahrens, and its Chief People Officer and Executive Vice President, Astronaut Operations, Aparna Chitale, enhancing certain severance and bonus provisions. The move underscores the company’s efforts to retain and protect key leadership amid potential corporate transitions, which could influence management stability and stakeholder confidence in periods of strategic change.

Under the revised terms, both executives are now entitled to receive any earned but unpaid annual bonus for the year prior to a qualifying termination of employment. In addition, if they experience a qualifying termination on or within 24 months after a change in control, Ahrens’ cash severance multiplier rises from 1.0 to 1.5 and subsidized healthcare coverage for both executives extends from 12 to 18 months, strengthening their protections in the event of leadership or ownership shifts.

data.title: Trinity Industries Expands Green Railcar Financing Through New Notes
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

The latest update is out from Trinity Industries ( (TRN) ).

On April 17, 2026, Trinity Rail Leasing 2025 LLC, an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Trinity Industries, issued $480.8 million of secured green standard railcar notes in two tranches, with fixed interest rates of 5.35% for Class A and 5.56% for Class B, both maturing in 2056 and secured by a portfolio of railcars and operating leases. The proceeds, along with prior issuances under the same indenture, will be used in part to redeem $377.1 million of 2019 railcar equipment notes and for general corporate purposes, reshaping Trinity’s railcar financing structure and shifting obligations into the TRL-2025 vehicle, with noteholders holding priority rights and enforcement powers tied to asset performance and collateral coverage.

TRL-2025’s new notes and previously issued 2025 series notes are backed by railcars acquired in multiple tranches from Trinity’s leasing subsidiaries, consolidating ownership and servicing of the railcar portfolio under a dedicated asset-backed platform. This transaction is expected to extend the duration and diversify the company’s funding base while maintaining off-balance-sheet, secured financing for key rail assets, though repayment timing remains dependent on future cash flows, asset values and adherence to amortization tests embedded in the financing structure.

An update from North American Construction Group ( (TSE:NOA) ) is now available.

On April 21, 2026, North American Construction Group announced that its Australian subsidiary MacKellar Group has amended and expanded a five-year contract with a leading metallurgical coal producer in Queensland, Australia. The original agreement, signed in August 2024, had already shifted equipment to fully maintained fleets and added an on-site maintenance facility, positioning MacKellar as a core contractor at the mine.

The revised contract, which still runs to September 30, 2029, adds additional fully maintained equipment and services expected to generate about $125 million in incremental revenue and boost MacKellar’s scope at the site by roughly 50%. The expanded work, starting May 1, 2026 and ramping up by August 2026, aligns with NACG’s 2026 financial guidance and underscores its disciplined capital deployment, with eight haul trucks pre-purchased in December 2025 and about $25 million of further fleet growth capital planned for 2026, reinforcing its Tier 1 contractor status in Australia.

Silvercorp Metals ( (TSE:SVM) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, Silvercorp Metals Inc. announced it had entered into a three-year syndicated term loan facilities agreement totaling RMB 1.5 billion (about US$220 million) with a group of international banks, led by Standard Chartered Bank (Hong Kong) Limited. The financing, split between a floating-rate tranche of RMB 425.5 million and a fixed-rate tranche of RMB 1.0475 billion and oversubscribed to RMB 2 billion in commitments, is repayable in RMB and will be used for general corporate purposes and global working capital.

The company said the new loan package, secured by guarantees and certain accounts and share pledges from Silvercorp and key subsidiaries, is structured with potential interest rate reductions tied to its consolidated net leverage ratio. Management framed the deal as a landmark transaction that complements Silvercorp’s existing cash reserves and operating cash flow, strengthening its balance sheet and financial flexibility as it advances long-term growth initiatives in its global mining portfolio, including projects in Kyrgyzstan and Ecuador.

data.title: Northfield Bancorp Swings to Profit in First Quarter
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Northfield Bancorp ( (NFBK) ) just unveiled an update.

Northfield Bancorp reported a sharp turnaround in its first-quarter 2026 results, released on April 20, 2026, posting net income of $11.8 million, or $0.30 per diluted share, versus a net loss of $27.4 million in the prior quarter and $7.9 million in the first quarter of 2025. The rebound reflected the absence of last quarter’s $41 million goodwill impairment, stronger net interest income driven by wider margins and lower funding costs, and a lower provision for credit losses, partially offset by higher merger-related and compensation expenses.

Net interest income rose to $37.0 million and net interest margin improved to 2.76% as yields on loans and mortgage-backed securities increased and the cost of interest-bearing liabilities fell, aided by a shift toward lower-cost deposits. Core deposits excluding brokered balances expanded by $83.3 million while loan balances, mainly in multifamily credits, declined by $48.8 million, asset quality remained solid with non-performing loans at 0.56% of total loans, and the board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.13 per common share payable on May 20, 2026, as the company advanced planning for its pending merger with Columbia Financial.

The most recent analyst rating on (NFBK) stock is a Hold with a $14.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Northfield Bancorp stock, see the NFBK Stock Forecast page.

SAP SE has convened its 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders for May 5, 2026, to be held as a virtual-only event in accordance with German stock corporation law, with shareholders participating remotely rather than in person. The agenda covers the presentation of already approved 2025 financial statements, a proposed dividend of €2.50 per share from 2025 retained earnings, allocation of additional reserves, discharge of the Executive and Supervisory Boards, appointment of auditors and sustainability-report auditors for 2026, approval of the 2025 compensation report, renewal and expansion of authorization to issue convertible and warrant-linked instruments with new contingent capital, amendments to enable electronic share issuance, and elections to the Supervisory Board, together signaling continued refinement of SAP’s capital structure, governance and shareholder engagement mechanisms.
